qa_matrix.question_text: You Vanka Veerasekhar, declare that, you have fully understood the questions asked to you as per this call and have furnished complete, true and accurate information after fully understanding the same. Sir/Mam' are you in agreement with the declaration that I have read out to you.
qa_matrix.captured_response: Okay sir. So, the questions here are related to the information you provided about your health and habits. ... And if the proposal is accepted, a legal contract will be formed on behalf of the company. If you give any wrong or false information, there are chances of cancellation even after the application is submitted. Okay, sir? So please give the correct answers after thinking, sir.

qa_matrix.question_text: In the last 24 months, have you smoked or used any tobacco or nicotine products?
qa_matrix.captured_response: Four years ago, I quit.

qa_matrix.question_text: Do you consume alcohol ?
qa_matrix.captured_response: Yes, I do.

qa_matrix.question_text: Please select the type of alcohol you consume. Spirit/Hard Liqour
qa_matrix.captured_response: Third one, third one. (implies hard liquor/spirit)

qa_matrix.question_text: In a week,How many unit of Hard liqour/spirit do you consume ?1 unit of alcohol =  1 glass of spirits (44 ml)
qa_matrix.captured_response: When in a party. ... One, two. ... 90ml you take? Yes, ma'am. Only on parties, right, sir? Otherwise, okay, like on a weekly basis, there's no habit, right? No such habit.
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: 1-2 pegs (90ml each) at parties, not weekly.

qa_matrix.question_text: Have you ever been advised by a doctor to reduce or stop alcohol consumption ?
qa_matrix.captured_response: No one told me.

qa_matrix.question_text: Have you ever used recreational drugs or Narcotics that was not prescribed by a Licensed physician ?
qa_matrix.captured_response: No, nothing like that.

qa_matrix.captured_response: 5.7 (likely feet, which is 170.18 cm)

High/Low blood pressure, raised cholesterol or triglycerides or lipids

qa_matrix.captured_response: No cancer-related issues, right sir? No, no. Precancerous ulcers, leukemia, enlarged lymph nodes. No, no. Leukoplakia, sarcoma, tumors, polyps, nodules, cysts, lumps. I am just listening to what you are saying, I don't have any of those currently.

qa_matrix.captured_response: No diabetes or anything like that, right sir? Raised blood sugar? No. Sugar in urine, hyperthyroidism, hypothyroidism, thyroid problems, nothing like that, right sir? Endocrine disorders. No, none.

qa_matrix.question_text: Asthma, bronchitis, sinusitis, tuberculosis, pneumonia, chronic or recurrent cough, sleep apnea, emphysema, chronic pulmonary disease (COPD) or any other respiratory disorders
qa_matrix.captured_response: Asthma, TB, bronchitis, sinusitis, pneumonia, recurrent cough, sleep apnea, you don't have any issues like these, right sir? Emphysema. No, no. Bronchitis.

qa_matrix.captured_response: Hepatitis B.

qa_matrix.captured_response: None of that. There is nothing like that. No lumps or anything. Positive alpha fetoprotein, AST, nothing like that, right?

qa_matrix.captured_response: Nothing.

qa_matrix.captured_response: There are no regular check-ups. I go to the hospital when I have a fever, otherwise I don't go. ... So related to Hepatitis, it was about two, three years ago? Yes, that's all. So you haven't consulted recently?

qa_matrix.question_text: Anemia, thalassemia, systemic lupus erythematosus or any other diseases or disorders of the blood or autoimmune disease

qa_matrix.question_text: Gastritis, gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), stomach ulcer, recurrent indigestion, irritable bowel syndrome, Barrett's esophagus, atrophic gastritis, Crohn's disease, ulcera-tive colitis, Pancreatitis/Pancreatic disorder or any gastrointestinal diseases or disorders

qa_matrix.captured_response: No.

qa_matrix.captured_response: Eight years ago, you had an ulcer, a stomach ulcer, sir? Yes. ... So, the last time you experienced these symptoms was also eight years ago, right? Nothing recently, right sir? Nothing.

qa_matrix.question_text: Kidney stones, gall stones, kidney or urinary tract infection, blood or protein in urine, chronic kidney disease, kidney failure, piles, fistula, hydrocele or any other disorders of the kidney, bladder, genital, prostate or urinary systems?

qa_matrix.captured_response: Back pain, if I sit for too long, I get back pain. ... So, not continuous, recurrent back pain, right sir? No, no. Normally, if I sit for too long, I get back pain.

qa_matrix.captured_response: Ear problems, ENT problems, nothing like that, right sir? ... Deafness, hearing, speech problems, hoarseness of voice, difficulty in swallowing, ear discharge, nothing like that. ... Disorders of ear, eyes, nose, throat.

qa_matrix.question_text: Is there anything else that you would like to share with us with respect to your health/ habits or anything else that you believe is important to ensure that policy is processed basis all facts & information?
qa_matrix.captured_response: No, nothing. That is, treatment means I took an injection. Treatment means they told me to take an injection, that's it.

qa_matrix.question_text: Have you fully recovered with no further symptoms, investigations, treatment or compli-cations?
qa_matrix.captured_response: Yes, normal fever, if it doesn't reduce, then... Hospitalized, that was once for a fever, it was just about the fever. ... Yes, if it didn't subside, then we got admitted to the hospital. Yes. One day.

qa_matrix.question_text: When you have recovered. Please confirm duration in months
qa_matrix.captured_response: almost five years ago, you had a general fever.

qa_matrix.question_text: Any abnormal medical findings or pending test results, scheduled or planned or pre-scribed medical tests such as imaging scan reports (e.g. x-ray, ultrasound, MRI, PET scan, CT scan etc.), biopsy, electrocardiogram (ECG), treadmill, Mammography, Endo-scopy/Colonoscopy, PAP Smear, blood or urine test
qa_matrix.captured_response: No, I haven't. Treadmill, nothing like that? Endoscopy, colonoscopy. That is, when I get a normal fever, they might ask for a blood test to check if it's a viral fever or something, that's all. Normally, I don't do anything else.

qa_matrix.question_text: Medication or treatment that lasted more than 2 weeks or had been referred to a specialist doctor or hospital for any treatment/consultation and/or investigations (eg: Blood, ECG, MRI, Xray etc)
qa_matrix.captured_response: No, I haven't. I only take it once. Whether it gets better or not, I don't take it again.

qa_matrix.question_text: Inability to perform your daily activities or absent from work more than two 2 consecutive weeks at a time
qa_matrix.captured_response: If I am unwell, I take it, but...

qa_matrix.captured_response: No, none.

qa_matrix.captured_response: No, none.

qa_matrix.captured_response: No, nothing. Nothing.

critical_issues: Incorrect data captured for Education Qualification: MER states 'Below Graduate' but customer is 'Graduate'.
Incorrect data captured for Current Occupation: MER states 'Salaried' but customer is 'working in chemicals, solo work'.
Incorrect data captured for Aadhaar ID: MER has a partial number '2220' while customer provided '35504330220'. The partial number in MER does not match the end of the full number provided.
Incorrect data captured for Alcohol Consumption: MER states '1 unit' weekly, but customer specified '1-2 pegs (90ml each)' only at parties, not weekly.
The doctor did not explicitly ask for agreement to the declaration at the end of reading it.
The doctor did not explicitly ask about 'Fatty liver' under Health.f, although it was an expected disclosure in the MER.
The doctor did not explicitly ask about 'Ulcerative Colitis' and 'Irritable bowel syndrome' under Health.i, although they were expected disclosures in the MER.

The doctor did not explicitly ask about all specific conditions listed under the open-ended 'Is there anything else' question (Additional.1), such as 'Annual Health Checkup', 'Viral infection', 'Dengue fever', 'Malaria', 'Mouth ulcer', 'Hay fever', 'COVID-19 Positive', 'Others'.
recommendations: Ensure all personal particulars (Education, Occupation, ID Proof) are accurately captured and match the customer's verbal responses.
Clarify and accurately record alcohol consumption frequency and quantity, ensuring it aligns with the MER's unit definition or note discrepancies.
Ensure the full declaration, including the explicit question for agreement, is read out and acknowledged by the customer.
For questions with multiple expected disclosures (e.g., Health.f, Health.i, Additional.1), the doctor should explicitly ask about each specific condition listed in the MER to ensure completeness.
Verify and correct any unit typos in the MER document, such as the height unit (5.7 cm should likely be 5.7 feet or converted to cm).

customer_satisfaction_indicators: Customer is cooperative and provides answers.
Customer clarifies information when prompted (e.g., about back pain, Hepatitis B, ulcer).

validation_errors: MER's height unit 'cm' for value '5.7' is likely a typo; assuming 5.7 feet (170.18 cm).
Discrepancy in alcohol consumption: MER states '1 unit' weekly, but customer states '1-2 pegs (90ml each)' only at parties, not weekly.
MER lists 'Fatty liver' as an expected disclosure under Health.f, but it was not mentioned by the customer or explicitly asked by the doctor.
MER lists 'Ulcerative Colitis' and 'Irritable bowel syndrome' as expected disclosures under Health.i, but they were not mentioned by the customer or explicitly asked by the doctor.

MER lists several specific conditions under 'Additional.1' (Is there anything else), but these were not explicitly asked by the doctor, and the customer did not volunteer them (except for general fever).
